channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by AI

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan coast guard expels Chinese ships from restricted waters
item.description: Taiwan's coast guard said it "expelled" four Chinese government ships that entered restricted waters around Taiwan, marking another escalation in maritime tensions in the Taiwan Strait.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: 🇨🇳🇯🇵🇵🇭 Beijing sends largest patrol ship east of Taiwan after Japan-Philippine boundary talks
item.description: China deployed its largest patrol vessel near Taiwan following Japan-Philippine maritime boundary discussions. Communist Party mouthpiece warned the two countries are becoming a "source of trouble" and putting regional peace at risk.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇫🇮🇪🇺🇷🇺 Finland's Stubb urges Europe to step up and lead peace talks with Russia
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb called for Europe to take a more active role in leading peace negotiations with Russia, as the continent grapples with how to resolve the ongoing conflict in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇬🇧⚓⚔️ UK Royal Navy's entire submarine fleet out of service for maintenance
item.description: Britain's entire fleet of Astute-class submarines are currently out of service waiting for maintenance, raising concerns about the UK's ability to deter Russian stealth submarines and protect its Vanguard fleet.
item.dc:creator: The Telegraph

item.title: 🇬🇧🏛️ Cabinet bodyguards working without security clearance
item.description: Bodyguards protecting senior UK Cabinet ministers have been operating without proper security clearance, according to a leaked letter raising serious concerns about ministerial safety.
item.dc:creator: The Telegraph

item.title: ✈️📉 Global airlines slash 2026 profit forecast on fuel shock from Iran war
item.description: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) downgraded its 2026 profit outlook for the global airline industry, citing fuel price volatility and geopolitical disruption from the Iran conflict. Profits are expected to halve despite resilient passenger demand.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping heads to North Korea with nuclear weapons off the agenda
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang for the first time in nearly seven years, signaling a strategic focus on North Korea as a regional partner. Kim Jong Un projects confidence with nuclear capabilities and Russian alliance, showing little appetite for Washington engagement.
item.dc:creator: The Economist

item.title: ⚽🇮🇷 Iran national soccer team arrives in Mexico for World Cup training
item.description: Iran squad lands in Mexico ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where they will prepare before group matches in the United States. The team faces US, England, and Ukraine in the group stage.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump rules out unfreezing Iranian assets before peace deal
item.description: In an NBC News interview, US President Donald Trump stated he would not unfreeze Iranian assets or lift sanctions until a comprehensive peace agreement is reached. The stance could complicate ongoing ceasefire negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Strikes Beirut Suburbs Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israel attacked Beirut's southern suburbs days after a US-brokered ceasefire, risking renewed regional escalation as Iran warned strikes on Beirut could restart full-scale war across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇮🇱 One killed, five wounded in central Israel terror shooting spree
item.description: A terrorist attack in communities bordering the West Bank north of Tel Aviv left one person dead and five wounded, with two in serious condition. One perpetrator was shot during the rampage.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: 🇺🇦 Bridge linking Crimea to southern Ukraine damaged in drone strike
item.description: A drone strike damaged a bridge near the village of Chonhar connecting occupied Crimea with Russian-controlled parts of southern Ukraine, impacting a key logistics route for Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Trump-Xi summit on Taiwan offered more than optics
item.description: The meeting between Chinese and US presidents laid the groundwork for more predictable China-US relations, including on the Taiwan issue, beyond just symbolic photo opportunities.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: ☢️🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russian Drone Strike Hits Nuclear Fuel Storage Near Chornobyl
item.description: A Russian drone damaged a storage facility for spent nuclear fuel near the Chornobyl plant, Zelensky condemned as "extremely vile." The IAEA confirmed the strike hit near where nuclear material is stored, with no radiation spike detected.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ☢️🇮🇷🇺🇸 US Demands Iran Disclose Nuclear Sites and Uranium Stocks at IAEA
item.description: A US draft resolution before the UN nuclear watchdog board calls on Iran to provide precise information on its bombed nuclear sites and enriched uranium stocks, escalating diplomatic pressure amid ongoing ceasefire tensions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇵🇪🗳️ Peru Votes in Tight Presidential Runoff Testing Latin America's Rightward Shift
item.description: Peruvians cast ballots in a close presidential runoff between pro-US conservative Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez, a race that could determine whether Latin America's rightward trend continues or reverses.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇦🇲🗳️ Armenians Vote in Pivotal Election Testing Westward Shift Amid Russian Pressure
item.description: Armenia holds parliamentary elections seen as a test of PM Pashinyan's pivot away from Russia, with six pro-Russian opposition candidates arrested the day before the vote.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷🤝 Pakistan's Interior Minister in Tehran to Mediate Iran-US Talks
item.description: Pakistan's Mohsin Naqvi visited Tehran carrying a special letter as regional powers push to revive Iran-US negotiations, while US forces downed additional Iranian drones over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping makes rare state visit to North Korea for first time in nearly seven years
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping will visit Pyongyang for talks with Kim Jong Un, projecting confidence and defiance as North Korea maintains its nuclear arsenal and alliance with Russia while showing little interest in engaging Washington.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 💰 US considers redirecting frozen Iranian assets to Gulf allies for reconstruction
item.description: The Trump administration is exploring using frozen Iranian assets to fund rebuilding efforts in Gulf states affected by the conflict, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled over Tehran demand for access to $24 billion in oil revenue.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🛡️ Russia downs 339 Ukrainian drones in massive 13-hour interception barrage
item.description: Russian air defenses intercepted and destroyed 339 Ukrainian drones over a 13-hour period across multiple regions including Moscow, in one of the largest drone attacks of the war.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇸 Russia signals openness to new US relationship as Ukraine war stalls
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has expressed openness to a new relationship with the United States, even as peace talks show no progress in ending the Ukraine conflict. The overture comes amid continued battlefield deadlock.
item.dc:creator: NBC News

item.title: ⚛️🔌 Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power supply after outage
item.description: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its power supply Saturday after an outage, according to plant management. The facility has faced repeated power disruptions during the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸⚔️ US troops and families adjust to prolonged state of conflict with Iran
item.description: American military personnel and their families are adapting to an unusual state of conflict with Iran that is neither full-scale war nor peace, with ongoing tensions causing stress for service members stationed in the region.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigerian military rescues 360 hostages from Borno mountain hideout
item.description: Security forces freed 360 men, women, and children held by kidnappers in a mountain enclave in Borno state, in a major operation against militant groups.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
